description: description
responsibilities: What You’ll Do: Developing and implementing in building scalable backend systems, APIs, and microservices. Instill best practices for software development, ensure designs meet requirements, and deliver high-quality work on schedule. Writing application backend codes using Python, SQL, NoSQL Troubleshooting backend application codes. Developing and implementing back-end architecture Documenting application changes and developing updates. What You’ll Bring: A master’s or bachelor’s degree in computer science or related field from a top university. 6-10 years' overall experience. Experience in building and leading strong engineering team. Strong proficiency in Python and experience with FastAPI or similar asynchronous frameworks (e.g., Flask, Django). Proven experience in building scalable backend systems, APIs, and microservices. Knowledge of AI/ML concepts and experience in integrating AI models into backend services is highly desirable. Familiarity with database technology such as SQL and NoSQL databases (optional). Experience with cloud services (AWS, GCP, Azure) and containerization technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is a plus. Good problem-solving skills and ability to work in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment. Experience in frontend technologies like React.js\Vue.js\Angular Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collabrative spirit and probelm-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: What You’ll Do: Experience with cloud technologies – AWS, Azure or GCP Create container images and maintain container registries. Create, update, and maintain production grade applications on Kubernetes clusters and cloud. Inculcate GitOps approach to maintain deployments. Create YAML scripts, HELM charts for Kubernetes deployments as required. Take part in cloud design and architecture decisions and support lead architects build cloud agnostic applications. Create and maintain Infrastructure-as-code templates to automate cloud infrastructure deployment Create and manage CI/CD pipelines to automate containerized deployments to cloud and K8s. Maintain git repositories, establish proper branching strategy, and release management processes. Support and maintain source code management and build tools. Monitoring applications on cloud and Kubernetes using tools like ELK, Grafana, Prometheus etc. Automate day to day activities using scripting. Work closely with development team to implement new build processes and strategies to meet new product requirements. Troubleshooting, problem solving, root cause analysis, and documentation related to build, release, and deployments. Ensure that systems are secure and compliant with industry standards. What You’ll Bring A master’s or bachelor’s degree in computer science or related field from a top university. 2-4+ years of hands-on experience in DevOps Hands-on experience designing and deploying applications to cloud (Aws / Azure/ GCP) Expertise on deploying and maintaining applications on Kubernetes Technical expertise in release automation engineering, CI/CD or related roles. Hands on experience in writing Terraform templates as IaC, Helm charts, Kubernetes manifestsShould have strong hold on Linux commands and script automation. Technical understanding of development tools, source control, and continuous integration build systems, e.g. Azure DevOps, Jenkins, Gitlab, TeamCity etc. Knowledge of deploying LLM models and toolchains Configuration management of various environments. Experience working in agile teams with short release cycles. Good to have programming experience in python / go. Characteristics of a forward thinker and self-starter that thrives on new challenges and adapts quickly to learning new knowledge. How you’ll grow: Cross-functional skills development & custom learning pathways Milestone training programs aligned to career progression opportunities Internal mobility paths that empower growth via s-curves, individual contribution and role expansions Hybrid working model: ZS is committed to a Flexible and Connected way of working. ZSers are onsite at clients or ZS offices three days a week. Combined flexibility to work remotely two days a week is also available. The magic of ZS culture and innovation thrives in both planned and spontaneous face-to-face connections

description: description
responsibilities: About the Expertise Centre – R&D Technologist The R&D Technologist Expertise Centre within ZS’s Drug Development practice partners with biopharma organizations to design and deliver innovative clinical data solutions that accelerate drug development and reduce risk from portfolio to patient. The team operates at the intersection of key ZS drug development verticals—including asset strategy, clinical trial design, clinical data management, statistical programming, regulatory, and risk-based quality. By combining deep clinical domain expertise, data standards, clinical programming, systems understanding, and consulting discipline, the team solves complex R&D challenges with speed, scale, and measurable impact. Director Expertise Center R&D Technologist – Clinical data solutions The Director – R&D Technologist (Clinical Data Solutions) provides strategic and scientific leadership across clinical data management, analytics, biostatistics, statistical programming, and clinical data standards. This role is central to the R&D Technologist Expertise Centre and focuses on designing and delivering innovative clinical data solutions across the drug development lifecycle—with a strong emphasis on clinical data standards, transformation, and analytics-driven decision-making. You will combine deep technical expertise, consulting leadership, and practice-building capabilities to serve as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, driving next-generation clinical data and analytics solutions that improve development outcomes and accelerate regulatory submissions What You’ll Do: Strategic & Client Leadership Provide strategic leadership across clinical data management, biometrics, and advanced analytics for end-to-end drug development programs Serve as ZS’s go-to leader for clinical data solutions, bringing a strong techno-functional perspective across clinical data flow and systems Lead complex client engagements spanning clinical data strategy, analytics enablement, and digital R&D transformation Facilitate senior-level workshops to align stakeholders on study design, data standards, analytics approaches, and operating models Clinical Data & Standards Leadership Bring deep expertise in clinical data standards (e.g., CDISC – CDASH, SDTM, ADaM, TLF) Define innovative approaches to clinical data collection, transformation, and analytics Integrate clinical programming, analytics, and data science into trial design, execution, and regulatory workflows Drive adoption of standardized, scalable, and future-ready clinical data models Technology & Innovation Apply a techno-functional lens across clinical systems, data platforms, and enterprise architectures Leverage AI/ML and GenAI approaches to transform clinical data processing and analytics Define future-state operating models and digital ecosystems for clinical data and analytics Delivery Excellence & Governance Oversee quality, scientific rigor, and compliance of deliverables, including analytics, data models, and regulatory outputs Ensure alignment with GxP standards, regulatory expectations, and industry best practices Act as a bridge between scientific, business, and engineering teams Team & Practice Building Lead and grow a high-performing clinical data and analytics team Mentor senior talent and establish standards for delivery excellence Contribute to capability building, accelerators, and new offerings within the Expertise Centre Support business development through solutioning, client pursuits, and long-term relationship building
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Experience & Domain Expertise 18+ years of experience in Life Sciences R&D, with strong expertise in clinical data management, biometrics, and analytics Deep understanding of the end-to-end drug development lifecycle Strong domain knowledge of clinical data, analytics, and digital R&D transformation Clinical Systems & Platforms Experience working with: EDC Platforms: Medidata Rave, Veeva EDC, Oracle InForm Clinical Systems: ePRO, laboratory systems, CTMS, eTMF, CDMS Clinical Data Repositories: SAS LSAF, Oracle LSH, eClinical Elluminate Metadata Repositories: Nurocor, Sycamore, Formedix Statistical Computing: SAS, SAS Viya, Domino Data Standards & Interoperability Strong expertise in: CDISC: CDASH, SDTM, ADaM, TLF Interoperability: FHIR, HL7, USDM Technical & Analytical Skills Working knowledge of R, Python, and SAS for clinical data transformation and analysis Exposure to AI/ML and GenAI-based solutions in clinical data and analytics Strong capability in business process modeling, data flow modeling, and solution architecture design Leadership & Consulting Skills Strong communication and stakeholder management skills with ability to influence senior leaders Proven experience in GxP-compliant, large-scale enterprise programs Ability to lead global, cross-functional, and multi-vendor teams Strong leadership, mentoring, and team-building capabilities Solid understanding of agile, product-led, and digital transformation delivery models

description: description
responsibilities: Knowledge Services Consultant – Pharma at ZS Knowledge Services Group Overview Knowledge Services group offers a broad spectrum of Business Research, Pre-Sales and other knowledge-based services to clients and internal stakeholders. Knowledge Services has emerged as one of ZS’s distinctive competitive advantages, and a key enabler for our consultants to have lasting impact while addressing their clients' sales & marketing issues. Our services are based on deep research, and we bring together a unique combination of Business Research, Life-Sciences and Clinical experiences. What You’ll Do Assess firm knowledge needs and requirements (short-term or long-term) Deliver targeted insights using internal knowledge and external sources Help the firm prepare materials for Summits and Conferences Support the creation and update of knowledge assets including client updates, competitive intelligence, industry research etc. Maintain, manage, and develop the firm’s knowledge infrastructure Share best practices and tactical knowledge across offices, practice areas and teams Engage and build relationships with firm members and leaders What You’ll Bring A track record in research and knowledge services, ready to support diverse ZS practice areas and project teams 72+ months of relevant knowledge services experience in IT/Consulting companies An academic background in life sciences, biotechnology, or related disciplines such as B.Sc (Life Sciences/Bioscience), B. Pharma, or an MBAF Experience in working on databases like Evaluate Pharma, Adis, Avention, Datamonitor, Nexis, etc. Proficiency in computer applications, including MS Office, with an interest in Enterprise applications/BI tools Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: ZS Data Office: The Data Office focuses on building relationships and partnerships with a wide range of data providers to enable ZS access and use of the data in innovative offerings and services. The Data Office Associate, based in Noida (India) will provide services to our Client Service Teams and Practice Areas. Post gaining a deep understanding of the healthcare data landscape, existing data partnerships, and internal processes, he/she will be advising ZS stakeholders on the potential data options to support ZS clients and internal projects while ensuring the compliant use of data. To deliver on our promise, the individual will collaborate closely with the broader team spread across Argentina, the United States, Europe, and India. What you’ll do: Develop an in-depth understanding and expertise in the global healthcare data landscape, including key data assets available at ZS and through our partnerships with data vendors Collaborate with ZS stakeholders, including client service teams and practice areas, to identify and translate their business needs into data requirements Maintain, manage, and develop the Data office’s knowledge infrastructure Manage and document requests and activities thoroughly. Execute reporting tasks to ensure accurate and timely delivery of information Collaborate with an extended global team of diverse skill sets (knowledge management, data technologists, business operations, etc.) Support research initiatives, internal projects, and marketing endeavors while ensuring seamless communication.
qualifications: What you’ll bring: MBA & B. Pharma/BDS/Life Sciences (or equivalent related disciplines), with a strong record of academic achievement 6-18 months of relevant job experience; working with consultancy firms, life science companies, or healthcare data providers preferred (applicable only for Market hires) Ability to translate unstructured problems into actionable requirements and approaches Self-starter, with high motivation, maturity, and personal initiative Strong oral and written communication skills Empathy, adaptability, and customer service orientation Self-discipline for planning and organizing tasks, and delivering in an unstructured environment Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach

role: Business Technology Solutions Manager - Patient Strategy & Services
description: description
responsibilities: We are looking for experienced leaders with strong Patient Support Services (PSS) and Salesforce CRM/PRM implementation expertise to lead large, complex patient services transformation programs. This role is accountable for end‑to‑end delivery, solution development, team leadership (including contractors and techno‑functional specialists), and growth of new client programs. The Manager sits at the intersection of strategy, solution architecture, delivery execution, and business development, working closely with senior client stakeholders, ZS leadership, and multi‑disciplinary delivery teams. The ideal candidate brings hands‑on implementation experience, a proven ability to build and lead diverse delivery teams, and demonstrated success in selling and scaling new programs across global life sciences clients. What You’ll Do Program Leadership & Delivery Ownership Own end‑to‑end delivery of large‑scale Patient CRM / PRM implementations on Salesforce Health Cloud and Life Sciences Cloud, from discovery through go‑live and hypercare. Lead multiple workstreams across enrollment, case management, reimbursement, patient engagement, nurse educator workflows, and AI‑enabled capabilities. Establish delivery governance including scope, roadmap, release planning, risk management, quality assurance, and executive reporting. Ensure solutions are scalable, compliant, and reusable across brands, markets, and therapeutic areas. Solution Development & Architecture Leadership Lead solution development by shaping end‑to‑end functional and technical designs in partnership with solution architects and engineering leads. Drive key design decisions across Health Cloud data models, service processes, integrations, security, and extensibility patterns. Ensure alignment between business objectives, Salesforce platform capabilities, and long‑term client technology strategy. Champion an OOTB‑first, accelerator‑driven approach to improve speed‑to‑value and consistency across programs. Team Leadership & Resource Management Build, lead, and mentor diverse delivery teams, including ZS consultants, offshore/onshore resources, contractors, and specialized techno‑functional roles. Own staffing strategy, onboarding, performance management, and capability development across programs. Foster strong collaboration across business analysts, architects, developers, QA, data/integration specialists, and change management teams. Stakeholder Management & Client Advisory Serve as the senior client advisor, engaging with executive stakeholders across business, IT, operations, and compliance. Lead executive‑level workshops to define future‑state operating models, KPIs, governance structures, and transformation roadmaps. Advise clients on best practices in patient services, reimbursement models, digital adherence, specialty and rare disease programs. Manage escalations, align expectations, and ensure sustained client satisfaction and value realization. Selling & Program Expansion Identify, shape, and sell new programs within existing and new client accounts. Lead or significantly contribute to pursuits, RFPs, and proposals, including solutioning, scope definition, estimates, resourcing models, and delivery approach. Partner with ZS leadership to grow long‑term client relationships and expand PSS technology footprint. Contribute to development of reusable assets, accelerators, and thought leadership to support go‑to‑market efforts. Data, Integration & Insights Leadership Provide oversight on integration strategy across HUB vendors, specialty pharmacies, EMR partners, and broader patient ecosystems. Guide data and reporting strategy for therapy initiation, adherence, operational performance, and patient outcomes. Ensure analytics and insights are embedded into solution design to support decision‑making and continuous improvement. What You’ll Bring 10–14 years of experience in technology consulting, with significant hands‑on Salesforce implementation experience. Proven track record leading end‑to‑end CRM/PRM implementations, preferably on Salesforce Health Cloud or Life Sciences Cloud. Strong background in life science/pharma essential. Experience and exposure to pharma patient services is preferable. This includes HUB operations, reimbursement, specialty therapies, oncology, or rare disease programs. Demonstrated experience managing large, diverse teams, including contractors and specialized techno‑functional roles. Proven ability to sell, scope, and grow new programs with senior client stakeholders. Excellent leadership, communication, and executive‑level presentation skills. Preferred Qualifications Salesforce certifications: Health Cloud Consultant, Life Sciences Cloud, or Architecture certifications. Experience working with large global pharma clients across multi‑brand and multi‑market programs. Exposure to AI‑driven, agentic, or automation‑led workflows in patient services or CRM ecosystems

role: Business Technology Solutions Associate Consultant - Data Engineer
description: description
responsibilities: What you’ll do: Undertake primary ownership in driving self and team effort across all phases of a project lifecycle. Translate business requirements into technical terms and drive team effort to design, build and manage technology solutions that solve business problems. Apply appropriate development methodologies (e.g. agile, waterfall) and best practices (e.g.: mid-development client reviews, embedded QA procedures, unit testing) to ensure successful and timely completion. Partner with Project lead/ Program lead in delivering projects and assist in project management responsibility like - project plans, people management, staffing and risk mitigation. Collaborate with other team members across the globe and ensure seamless communication and sharing/ undertaking tasks. Manage team of diverse skill sets (programmer, cloud analyst, BI developer, reporting, operations, etc.); Mentor/coach the junior members in the team. Lead task planning and task distribution across team members and ensure timely completion with high quality and report accurate status to seniors. Lead the project deliverables such as business case development, solution vision and design, user requirements, solution mockup, prototypes, and technical architecture, test cases, deployment plans, operations strategy and planning, etc.. Manage direct / indirect client communications as per the assigned responsibility in the project. Exhibit outlook to bring continuous improvement, innovation and exhibit capability to learn new areas (technology, business domain, project management process) and drive the similar effort of team members. Understand the problem statement, client requirements and design and build complex solutions using Programing languages, Big data service platform, etc. Ability to take right and timely decision on problem solving by considering broader picture, especially on approach, technical nuances and planning.
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Bachelor's/Master's degree with specialization in Computer Science, MIS, IT or other computer related disciplines. 4-6 years of relevant consulting-industry experience working on medium-large scale technology solution delivery engagements. At least 2 to 3 year of hands-on experience on Big data - Hive, Spark, Hadoop, MapReduce. Strong in at least one of the Programming languages - Python, Java, Scala, etc. and Programming basics - Data Structures. Strong understanding and hands on experience in RDBMS and NoSQL. Good handle on working in distributed computing and cloud services platform (but not limited to) - AWS, Azure, GCP. Additional Skills: Deep understanding of data management best practices and data analytics. Up to speed with latest technologies related (but not limited to) to cloud platform services, visualization, data management. Excellent verbal and written communication, with appropriate and logical structuring of thoughts process during discussion/ presentation. Lead project teams in driving end to end activities to meet set milestones and provide necessary mentorship/ guidance for the team growth. Ability to distill complex concepts into easy-to-understand frameworks and presentations. Strong verbal and written communication skills with ability to articulate results and issues to internal and client teams. Ability to work within a virtual global team environment and contribute to the overall timely delivery of multiple projects. Willingness to travel to other global offices as needed to work with client or other internal project teams.

role: Business Technology Solutions Associate - Data Engineer
description: description
responsibilities: Business Technology ZS’s Technology group focuses on scalable strategies, assets and accelerators that deliver to our clients enterprise-wide transformation via cutting-edge technology. We leverage digital and technology solutions to optimize business processes, enhance decision-making, and drive innovation. Our services include, but are not limited to, Digital and Technology advisory, Product and Platform development and Data, Analytics and AI implementation. What you’ll do: Business Technology Solutions Associate in Business Technology will Collaborate with ZS internal teams and client teams to shape and implement high quality technology solutions that address critical business problems Understand and analyze business problems thoroughly, and translate them into technical designs effectively Design and implement technical features using best practices for the specific technology stack being used Assist in the development phase of implementing technology solutions for client engagements, ensuring effective problem-solving Apply appropriate development methodologies (e.g., agile, waterfall, system integrated testing, mid-development client reviews, embedded QA procedures, unit testing) to ensure successful and timely completion of projects Provide guidance and support to team members in creating comprehensive project implementation plans Work closely with a development team to accurately interpret and implement business requirements
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business Analytics, Computer Science, MIS or related field with academic excellence Proficiency in RDBMS concepts, SQL, and programming languages such as Python Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to convert intricate business requirements into technology solutions Knowledge of algorithms and data structures Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach Additional Skills: 0-3+ years of relevant professional experience in delivering small/medium-scale technology solutions Strong verbal and written communication skills to effectively convey results and issues to internal and client teams Familiarity with Big Data Concepts and Cloud Platforms like AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform Understanding of productivity tools such as co-pilot and SQL generation Travel to other offices as required to collaborate with clients or internal project teams
